name: Homepage
description: Main landing page — hero imagery, featured events, things to do, neighborhoods, trip planning CTAs.
rebuildNotes: Custom layout. Pulls from multiple content types (events, listings, neighborhoods). Highest-visibility page — needs strong performance and visual impact.

name: Event Detail
description: Individual event pages grouped by category — music, art, theatre, festivals, sports, dance, outdoor recreation, and more.
rebuildNotes: Single template serving ~1,800 events. Data-driven from CMS. Must handle recurring events, multi-day events, and past/future states. Category taxonomy has 15+ categories.

name: Event Category Index
description: Category landing pages for events — filterable grid by date, category, and location.
rebuildNotes: Search/filter UX is the core challenge. Need fast filtering across ~1,800 events. Consider faceted search.

name: Listing/Business Detail
description: Business and attraction pages — restaurants, hotels, shops, services. Many businesses have multiple location entries.
rebuildNotes: Single template serving ~2,000 listings. Data from Simpleview CRM. Multi-location businesses create separate pages per location. Need to handle data quality variability.

name: Category Index
description: Filterable listing pages for browsing things to do, restaurants, attractions by category.
rebuildNotes: Similar UX to event index but for listings. Core search/filter experience. Needs to be fast with 2,000+ listings.

name: Blog / Article
description: Editorial content — blog posts, stories, and press articles. Two separate content areas that may be consolidatable.
rebuildNotes: Two existing content hubs (blog + articles) — consider consolidating into one. ~460 total posts to migrate. Standard CMS content type.

name: Neighborhood / Area Profile
description: Neighborhood and geographic area profile pages with local listings, character descriptions, and area guides.
rebuildNotes: Hierarchical geographic structure: regions → cities → neighborhoods. Each level has listings and events. Map integration important.

name: Plan Your Visit
description: Trip planning content — itineraries, transportation guides, accessibility, seasonal tips, and about Salt Lake City.
rebuildNotes: Mix of static content and dynamic itineraries. Consider whether itinerary builder is Phase 1 or Phase 2 scope.

name: Meetings & Convention
description: Convention and meetings sales content — venue information, services, planning tools, and RFP forms.
rebuildNotes: Functions as mini-sites within the main domain. Convention center pages are a high-value lead generation tool. RFP form integration critical.

name: Places to Stay
description: Hotel and accommodation pages — search, filter, and detail views for lodging options.
rebuildNotes: Likely pulls from Simpleview's lodging database. Booking integration may need third-party API. Smaller section but high commercial value.

name: Sports
description: Sports venues, teams, and sporting events content.
rebuildNotes: Small section. Mix of static content and venue profiles. May integrate with event detail template.

name: Hospitality Jobs
description: Job board for hospitality industry positions in the Salt Lake area.
rebuildNotes: Separate content type in CMS. Low complexity — standard listing template. Consider whether to keep or replace with third-party job board.

name: Static / Corporate Pages
description: About, membership, press room, research data, and travel trade information — corporate/organizational pages.
rebuildNotes: Standard CMS pages. Member area may need authentication. Press room needs media asset management. Travel trade section serves B2B audience.

name: Search Results
rebuildNotes: Needs to search across all content types (events, listings, articles, pages). Consider Algolia, Typesense, or similar for fast search.

name: 404 / Error Page
rebuildNotes: Standard implementation. Important for SEO — ensure proper 404 status codes and redirect strategy for migrated URLs.
